name: John Hickenlooper
subject: it’s absolute lunacy
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY HICKENLOOPER FOR COLORADO
body:
Trump and the GOP made lofty promises during the election, but what have they actually done for working people?

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113921820/565370885/1435897075?refcode=email_082425_priorities&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NTQ2NWMxNy0zZDgxLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UdAqIngNMS1dO4LzPOL8xCM57tu1cHqY4ud2Ku56ckg=&emci=3211a106-727f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=65465c17-3d81-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=7280715) Folks, over the course of last year’s elections, Trump and Washington Republicans made many lofty promises about bringing down prices, fixing the economy, and bringing jobs back to American soil. But since taking office, what have they actually done for working people? **_Let’s take a look:_** Donald Trump incited a global trade war, pushing our economy toward a RECESSION with shifting deadlines and the constant seesaw of on-again off-again tariffs. The so-called Department of Government Efficiency gutted agencies across the federal government, laying off thousands of workers critical to maintaining public lands, medical research, _and even the folks tasked with ensuring seniors get their Social Security checks on time._ **And now, Trump and the Republicans just passed and signed into law a budget that will likely kick 15 million Americans off their health care, shutter thousands of rural hospitals, and add $4 trillion – that’s FOUR TRILLION DOLLARS – to our debt,_just to pay for a lavish tax handout to the wealthiest among us._** It’s absolute lunacy. **But I promise you this: we're doing everything I can in the Senate to deliver for _you._** I'm fighting to lower the cost of living, protect your access to health care, and actually create more good-paying jobs here in the U.S. — _instead of just talking about it._ I’m bringing folks together to address the Colorado River crisis — _like I have since I was Governor_ — and passing bipartisan legislation that’ll help keep water flowing to all seven basin states. And I’m going to keep fighting tooth and nail to stop any and all Republican schemes to sell our public lands — _just like how we stripped it out of the recent GOP budget_ — because public lands belong in public hands, not auctioned off to the highest bidder. **[My top priority is and always will be serving the people of Colorado — no matter what Trump and the Republicans throw our way. If you’re with me, please, chip in a couple bucks to defend this seat and keep me in the fight for Colorado’s values in Washington. →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113921821/565370886/1506335976?refcode=email_082425_priorities&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NTQ2NWMxNy0zZDgxLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UdAqIngNMS1dO4LzPOL8xCM57tu1cHqY4ud2Ku56ckg=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | | [Donate $5 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113921822/565370887/1506335976?express_lane=true&amounts=5,15,25,50,100&amount=5&refcode=email_082425_priorities&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NTQ2NWMxNy0zZDgxLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UdAqIngNMS1dO4LzPOL8xCM57tu1cHqY4ud2Ku56ckg=)

clean_body: Trump and the GOP made lofty promises during the election, but what have they actually done for working people? Folks, over the course of last year’s elections, Trump and Washington Republicans made many lofty promises about bringing down prices, fixing the economy, and bringing jobs back to American soil. But since taking office, what have they actually done for working people? Let’s take a look: Donald Trump incited a global trade war, pushing our economy toward a RECESSION with shifting deadlines and the constant seesaw of on-again off-again tariffs. The so-called Department of Government Efficiency gutted agencies across the federal government, laying off thousands of workers critical to maintaining public lands, medical research, and even the folks tasked with ensuring seniors get their Social Security checks on time. And now, Trump and the Republicans just passed and signed into law a budget that will likely kick 15 million Americans off their health care, shutter thousands of rural hospitals, and add $4 trillion – that’s FOUR TRILLION DOLLARS – to our debt,just to pay for a lavish tax handout to the wealthiest among us. It’s absolute lunacy. But I promise you this: we're doing everything I can in the Senate to deliver for you. I'm fighting to lower the cost of living, protect your access to health care, and actually create more good-paying jobs here in the U.S. — instead of just talking about it. I’m bringing folks together to address the Colorado River crisis — like I have since I was Governor — and passing bipartisan legislation that’ll help keep water flowing to all seven basin states. And I’m going to keep fighting tooth and nail to stop any and all Republican schemes to sell our public lands — just like how we stripped it out of the recent GOP budget — because public lands belong in public hands, not auctioned off to the highest bidder. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | = | = | | | = | = | | | = | = Thank you, John | Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. # [District Round‐up 8.24.25](https://public-api.wordpress.com/bar/?stat=groovemails-events&bin=wpcom_email_click&redirect_to=http%3A%2F%2Fbethfarnhamforcongress.com%2F2025%2F08%2F24%2Fdistrict-round-up-8-24-25%2F%3Fjetpack_skip_subscription_popup&sr=0&signature=482b82460558da10b645cd642a2fb122&blog_id=213069660&user=248418217&_&_z=z)
| | By **Elizabeth Farnham** on August 24, 2025
---|---
Dear Neighbor,
Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been and what issues I’ve tackled in the past week. Criss-crossing the counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, Perry, Cumberland, and Somerset gives me great opportunities to meet with voters, hear what is on your hearts and minds, and the chance to voice my fight for Everyday Americans. If you would like to contribute to my campaign, please donate here: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beth-fa,rnham-for-congress-1](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=6218351802a8e0b98136c7ffb5829fac841acd5963938324cbd9efa3730a2420&blog_id=213069660&post_id=5076&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=ea74979466159707fa13f7561ea82084&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYWN0Ymx1ZS5jb20vZG9uYXRlL2JldGgtZmFybmhhbS1mb3ItY29uZ3Jlc3MtMQ=&email_id=8ad94cd7076cf326128ca74cdc355e2f)
Official Launch of the 2026 Beth Farnham for Congress Campaign!
Yesterday, Team Beth Farnham for Congress launched the official 2026 campaign at the Franklin County Visitors Center in Chambersburg. I was honored and thrilled to receive your support at this event. Franklin County Commissioner Bob Ziobrowski kindly introduced me and you enthusiastically clapped for many points I made during my speech.
I thank you all for your support as I fight for Everyday Americans. If you missed the event, here is the video to Bob's and my speeches. Many thanks to Campaign Manager Jack Stansbury for having recorded it.
Many thanks also to everyone who made this event possible including Team Beth Farnham for Congress for setting up, managing, and tearing down the event, and members of the Women's Democratic Club of Franklin County for providing refreshments.
[ ](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=6b341ded4f51589d14dc7c441c92510e749718bb28338601e6162f39115ef939&blog_id=213069660&post_id=5076&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=e756553fdf1f1f931a3b255ff41fb4c2&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly95b3V0dS5iZS9MZmhuU0dxRHZYTQ=&email_id=8ad94cd7076cf326128ca74cdc355e2f) [ ](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=6b341ded4f51589d14dc7c441c92510e749718bb28338601e6162f39115ef939&blog_id=213069660&post_id=5076&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=e756553fdf1f1f931a3b255ff41fb4c2&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly95b3V0dS5iZS9MZmhuU0dxRHZYTQ=&email_id=8ad94cd7076cf326128ca74cdc355e2f)
---
If you would rather read the speech, here it is:
Thank you, Bob.
Thank you All for coming out today to celebrate the launch of the Beth Farnham for Congress 2026 campaign.
I am honored and privileged to run for the office of US Representative for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District.
It is not only one of the largest geographic districts in Pennsylvania, it is the most beautiful - from Gettysburg to Johnstown, from Altoona to Shippensburg, from the Appalachian mountains to the Susquehanna River, there is rich history, rural culture, and stunning views everywhere you look.
Part of the privilege to run for this office comes from living here, but another part comes from the tremendous support that I receive from many of you, starting with my family.
Every morning, when the news stories threaten to disorient us, it is the heartfelt conversations with my dear husband Rob that steady and ready me for the day.
No matter what is happening at the federal level, how it affects our single income family directly, and what the future of this country, as well as that of public education looks like for our children, Laura and Daniel, I have peace because Rob and I are well-aligned politically and that includes our journey from rather bumbling Republican conservatives to the very progressive Democrats we are today.
Once upon a time, Rob and I voted for Republicans, not having studied the issues nor the candidates for that matter, but assumed that local Republicans stood for lower taxes and public education, until one day, we stopped our assumptions cold.
Even though we had a baby in 2015, the candidacy of Donald Trump landed on our radar and repulsed us both.
In 2016, we finally did our research, discovered what a competent, educated, experienced, candidate former Secretary of State Hilary Clinton was, and proudly voted for her.
We even brought the children to our polling place on that historic day.
Alas, her presidency was not to be, but we kept voting for Democrats anyway, as the Republicans only seemed to cede more and more of their power to Trump.
Until one day, I registered as a proud Democrat, and a few months later, so did Rob.
After our morning chats, I then move on to the work of my campaign and this most often involves working with the exceptional volunteers of Team Beth Farnham for Congress.
Because of their commitment, I can count on Barb Fisher as Treasurer, Jack Stansbury as Campaign Manager, Maribeth Fish, Sandy Hartzel, and Jack as Calendar Coordinators, Ike Loften, Claire Meissner, and Christina Palensky as the social media team, and Jay Inman as boots on the ground in Blair County.
They don't simply fill their roles, they crush them.
Their diligence and conscientiousness bring a consistency of message and presence across the ten plus counties and beyond.
Together, we are Team Beth Farnham for Congress and I could not be more proud
But we are not the only people who support this candidacy. I thank members of the Women’s Democratic Club of Franklin County for their tasty contributions here, but also I also thank the Democratic Committees of Blair, Cambria, Franklin, Bedford, Huntingdon, Juniata, Mifflin, Perry, Somerset, and Adams Counties who have already given me opportunities to speak at their events this year.
Additionally, Indivisible Blair County, Pennsylvania Federation of Democratic Women of Blair County, and Blue Storm Action have also welcomed me to speak this year at their marches, rallies, town hall, and meetings.
And all of us number among the over 104k people who voted for me last November, for which I thank you from the bottom of my heart.
I will not forsake your votes, but we will build upon them for no matter how we are registered to vote, no matter our background, gender expression, race, economic status, education level, religion, we are UNITED.
We are united in grief over the erosion of our precious Democracy, anger over those legislators who have the power to stop it, but won’t, fear about threats to us, our loved ones, our neighbors, our jobs, benefits, our rights over our own bodies, right to vote, this higher cost of living, our international reputation, and our national security.
We are also united in our demand to be represented by someone who is accessible, willing to not only attend your Town Halls, but to hold them myself, to answer your phone calls, emails, and letters, to vote in our best interests on bills in the House of Representatives that seek to strengthen our agency over our own bodies, support gun safety, defend public education, promote healthcare, and reform immigration, and to relate to us as Everyday Americans whom I define as hard-working families, retirees on a fixed income, veterans, young adults just making their way, and everyone who is not a multi-millionaire.
Yet, most of all, we are united in our resolve to preserve our Precious Democracy, our self-governance, our Constitution of the United States of America.
You see, while many of our neighbors have been voting for Republicans, mistaking lies for truth about what those Republicans will do for us in Congress, the ultra-wealthy, like Representative John Joyce have been voting against our interests.
Representative Joyce voted against the Infrastructure Bill, the American Rescue Plan, the Inflation Reduction Act, and the Women’s Health Protection Act.
Had more members of Congress voted like him, the sorely needed Infrastructure Bill wouldn’t have passed, but mercifully, Democrats carried the votes and Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District received the help it needed like lead-free pipes in Bellwood Borough of Joyce’s home county of Blair, updates to the airports in Cambria County, the bridge replacement in Franklin County, and rural broadband access in Adams, Bedford, Huntingdon, Juniata, Mifflin, and Perry counties.
Recently, Representative Joyce voted not once, but twice for the One Big Beautiful Bill Act that not only removes healthcare from 17,273 PA-13 residents and food assistance from 6,626 PA-13 residents over the next ten years, but with the cuts to the National Institute of Health (NIH), loses 374 jobs and $86M from our district over the same timeframe.
In other words, Representative Joyce does not care about us Everyday Americans - how could he?
In a congressional district where over 46% of the public school students qualify for free and reduced lunches, multimillionaire John Joyce is clearly not one of us, but if he won’t have the decency to resign from his disgrace, then it is up to us, the Everyday Americans, to vote him out and vote me in.
For when I take this oath of office, I will actually abide by it because I share a love of country with every veteran, every union member, and every activist who ever sacrificed in the name of Democracy.
I will stand with my right hand raised and say with all of my heart,
“I do solemnly swear (or affirm) that I will support and defend the Constitution of the United States against all enemies, foreign and domestic; that I will bear true faith and allegiance to the same; that I take this obligation freely, without any mental reservation or purpose of evasion; and that I will well and faithfully discharge the duties of the office on which I am about to enter: So help me God.”
And I do ask for God’s help because I am firm in my faith that we should feed the hungry, heal the sick, house the unhoused, welcome the stranger, and help the poor.
To stuff the pockets of the ultra-wealthy with tax savings that increase our federal deficit by $3 trillion dollars while depriving the least among us is well, not to put too fine a point on it, like trying to thread a camel through the eye of a needle.
Instead, as a member of Congress, I will always check the illegal actions of a president who violates the Constitution by illegally impounding congressionally-approved funds.
I won’t let a US President accept a $400 million dollar jet, much less for such an important vehicle as Air Force One.
No due process for people suspected by Immigration and Customs Enforcement?
Not on my watch.
I will do my due diligence and vote FOR Everyday Americans, not against us.
In other words, vote for me and I’ll vote for you.
When our neighbors, friends, family, and coworkers who voted differently than we did in November of last year, bear the consequences of those votes through job loss, lack of funding, the rising cost of living, the depressed values of our assets, the losses of healthcare, the rise of poverty, and the blight that accompanies it all, they will start to lift their heads and seek real solutions.
And in this constitutional crisis, we rise to meet that moment,
If there is any message I ask you to please take away from today, it is simply this - Amplify this campaign.
Because in the midst of so much darkness, we, standing together today, are the light.
Like the torch in the hand of the Statue of Liberty, we are on the right side of history and the right side of humanity.
We believe in healthcare, food assistance, due process, humane treatment of huddled masses, stewardship of the earth, public education, women’s rights, gun safety, and so much more.
When yet another cloud of fascism threatens to dim our stability, I ask you to shine for our neighbors in PA-13 because soon, they will be looking where to turn, and we, we will light that path for them by our continued rallies, protests, town halls, doorknocking, calling voters, letters to the editors, reposting social media, or donations to this campaign and of those who also hold dear our same beliefs.
When they understand whom to vote FOR, then they can finally understand that our US Representative can truly answer us, help us, and ultimately, represent US.
And I say this not as someone who has lofty ideals built from thin air, but as someone who lived the experience as a former registered Republican who saw the light and voted against the darkness of fascism, suppression of equality, violence, ignorance, disease, and propaganda.
When they join us in choosing me, Beth Farnham, then we all choose the best interest of PA-13 residents, the best interests of Everyday Americans, and ultimately, what the Revolutionary Founders gave to us - we too
Choose Democracy.
Thank you.
Earlier in the week, I attended the Juniata County Democratic Committee picnic where I met with many voters and heard what was on their hearts and minds. Later, I spoke to them about my fight for Everyday Americans, including the rising cost of living due to an incompetent tariff-by-tweet policy and impending job, healthcare, and food assistance losses as a result of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act that Representative John Joyce voted for twice.
Because part of the fight for Everyday Americans includes fighting for Affordable Housing, I attended the Landlord Summit on Tuesday.
[](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=66f4e7f6b1638f9d96b07cc2d468b7c8bceb92c7a6cf6b2cc89b0e202b85b8a9&blog_id=213069660&post_id=5076&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=b483783470b814d1bdd55aaaaa8f67f7&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url&email_id=8ad94cd7076cf326128ca74cdc355e2f)You see, until Congress authorizes a livable federal minimum wage, Everyday Americans are stuck paying exorbitant costs in rent, mortgages, taxes, and/or interest. The cost of housing is so far out of balance with income that programs exist to offset this tremendous burden for those least able to afford it.
Frankly, housing is a basic human right and so these programs shouldn’t have to exist in the richest nation in the world, but here we are.
I truly appreciate the informative presentation that I, and others, received from Amanda with the Franklin County Housing Authority. Because of her vast knowledge and the knowledge of others who work with her, I, and Campaign Manager, Jack Stansbury, were able to learn about the Housing Choice Voucher Program from the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development, the Franklin County Community Connections Access Center, Service Access & Management, Inc. which also includes Rapid Rehoming, Bridge Rental Assistance Subsidy, Veterans Affairs Supportive Housing, and information about the Public Housing available in Franklin County.
The hard work that many of these workers do to serve the community often goes unnoticed, but I thank them for giving me a greater understanding of the local resources available for families in need.
Some people celebrate the One Big Beautiful Bill Act that a convicted felon signed into law on July 4th of this year in terms of housing. For example, (OBBBA) provides a long overdue expansion of the Low-Income Housing Tax Credit, which funds a majority of new affordable housing projects in the United States.
Unfortunately, OBBBA’s “...$3 trillion bill’s devastating cuts to Medicaid and SNAP, enacted to free up funds for tax cuts for the ultra-rich, will bring about widespread job loss and a surge in poverty, causing the number of people who cannot afford housing to soar far beyond what the new housing provisions could address. (That is especially true in this era of surging construction costs thanks to tariffs and the decimation of the construction workforce.)”
Moreover, “Making Medicaid harder to get will disrupt the process of getting people out of homelessness, notes the Urban Institute, as well as remove a major funding source for supportive housing.
Medicaid cuts mean nursing homes will close, turning people out onto the streets, or sending them home to family caregivers...”
In other words, depriving people of Medicaid benefits, SNAP benefits, Medicare benefits, etc. like our own US Representative, multimillionaire John Joyce, voted for twice, only adds to the workload, but with fewer resources, that we learned about yesterday.
Of course, OBBBA can be reversed, but only by members of Congress willing to do it. I am one of those people, so please elect me, Beth Farnham, in 2026.
Together, we [#ChooseDemocracy](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=1d2b9d526664f561de2e63e2c879cdf510726f8c9a1d43e12c78ea8202e7a6b5&blog_id=213069660&post_id=5076&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=eec7ad79a2ec370539c4fb189d95ef4c&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=8ad94cd7076cf326128ca74cdc355e2f) and Affordable Housing!
[https://shelterforce.org/.../trumps-big-ugly-bill-is-a.../](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.wordpress.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=a740ac1776de351adae55411c41e3d203f8b99a18ef46997d2df7752721968b9&blog_id=213069660&post_id=5076&user_id=248418217&subs_id=487468588&signature=601e08fdd000e604ac5ad9dbf8686d30&email_name=new-post&user_email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9zaGVsdGVyZm9yY2Uub3JnLy4uLi90cnVtcHMtYmlnLXVnbHktYmlsbC1pcy1hLi4uLw=&email_id=8ad94cd7076cf326128ca74cdc355e2f)
(photo credit: Jack Stansbury)
If you’re like me, you love this country as much as I do and work to make a positive difference. In the midst of this Constitutional Crisis, please consider reaching out to your local Democratic committees or Democratic candidates so that we can strengthen our Democracy instead of letting it slide into fascism.
We don’t just #ChooseDemocracy, we choose Pennsylvania, we choose The United States of America, we choose Humanity and I am deeply glad to work along side you.
Sincerely,
Beth

clean_body: | Dear Neighbor, Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been and what issues I’ve tackled in the past week. Criss-cros... | or | | | or ---|---|--- | | By Elizabeth Farnham on August 24, 2025 ---|--- Dear Neighbor, Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been and what issues I’ve tackled in the past week. Criss-crossing the counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, Perry, Cumberland, and Somerset gives me great opportunities to meet with voters, hear what is on your hearts and minds, and the chance to voice my fight for Everyday Americans. If you would like to contribute to my campaign, please donate here: Official Launch of the 2026 Beth Farnham for Congress Campaign! Yesterday, Team Beth Farnham for Congress launched the official 2026 campaign at the Franklin County Visitors Center in Chambersburg. I was honored and thrilled to receive your support at this event. Franklin County Commissioner Bob Ziobrowski kindly introduced me and you enthusiastically clapped for many points I made during my speech. I thank you all for your support as I fight for Everyday Americans. If you missed the event, here is the video to Bob's and my speeches. Many thanks to Campaign Manager Jack Stansbury for having recorded it. Many thanks also to everyone who made this event possible including Team Beth Farnham for Congress for setting up, managing, and tearing down the event, and members of the Women's Democratic Club of Franklin County for providing refreshments. If you would rather read the speech, here it is: Thank you, Bob. Thank you All for coming out today to celebrate the launch of the Beth Farnham for Congress 2026 campaign. I am honored and privileged to run for the office of US Representative for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District. It is not only one of the largest geographic districts in Pennsylvania, it is the most beautiful - from Gettysburg to Johnstown, from Altoona to Shippensburg, from the Appalachian mountains to the Susquehanna River, there is rich history, rural culture, and stunning views everywhere you look. Part of the privilege to run for this office comes from living here, but another part comes from the tremendous support that I receive from many of you, starting with my family. Every morning, when the news stories threaten to disorient us, it is the heartfelt conversations with my dear husband Rob that steady and ready me for the day. No matter what is happening at the federal level, how it affects our single income family directly, and what the future of this country, as well as that of public education looks like for our children, Laura and Daniel, I have peace because Rob and I are well-aligned politically and that includes our journey from rather bumbling Republican conservatives to the very progressive Democrats we are today. Once upon a time, Rob and I voted for Republicans, not having studied the issues nor the candidates for that matter, but assumed that local Republicans stood for lower taxes and public education, until one day, we stopped our assumptions cold. Even though we had a baby in 2015, the candidacy of Donald Trump landed on our radar and repulsed us both. In 2016, we finally did our research, discovered what a competent, educated, experienced, candidate former Secretary of State Hilary Clinton was, and proudly voted for her. We even brought the children to our polling place on that historic day. Alas, her presidency was not to be, but we kept voting for Democrats anyway, as the Republicans only seemed to cede more and more of their power to Trump. Until one day, I registered as a proud Democrat, and a few months later, so did Rob. After our morning chats, I then move on to the work of my campaign and this most often involves working with the exceptional volunteers of Team Beth Farnham for Congress. Because of their commitment, I can count on Barb Fisher as Treasurer, Jack Stansbury as Campaign Manager, Maribeth Fish, Sandy Hartzel, and Jack as Calendar Coordinators, Ike Loften, Claire Meissner, and Christina Palensky as the social media team, and Jay Inman as boots on the ground in Blair County. They don't simply fill their roles, they crush them. Their diligence and conscientiousness bring a consistency of message and presence across the ten plus counties and beyond. Together, we are Team Beth Farnham for Congress and I could not be more proud But we are not the only people who support this candidacy. I thank members of the Women’s Democratic Club of Franklin County for their tasty contributions here, but also I also thank the Democratic Committees of Blair, Cambria, Franklin, Bedford, Huntingdon, Juniata, Mifflin, Perry, Somerset, and Adams Counties who have already given me opportunities to speak at their events this year. Additionally, Indivisible Blair County, Pennsylvania Federation of Democratic Women of Blair County, and Blue Storm Action have also welcomed me to speak this year at their marches, rallies, town hall, and meetings. And all of us number among the over 104k people who voted for me last November, for which I thank you from the bottom of my heart. I will not forsake your votes, but we will build upon them for no matter how we are registered to vote, no matter our background, gender expression, race, economic status, education level, religion, we are UNITED. We are united in grief over the erosion of our precious Democracy, anger over those legislators who have the power to stop it, but won’t, fear about threats to us, our loved ones, our neighbors, our jobs, benefits, our rights over our own bodies, right to vote, this higher cost of living, our international reputation, and our national security. We are also united in our demand to be represented by someone who is accessible, willing to not only attend your Town Halls, but to hold them myself, to answer your phone calls, emails, and letters, to vote in our best interests on bills in the House of Representatives that seek to strengthen our agency over our own bodies, support gun safety, defend public education, promote healthcare, and reform immigration, and to relate to us as Everyday Americans whom I define as hard-working families, retirees on a fixed income, veterans, young adults just making their way, and everyone who is not a multi-millionaire. Yet, most of all, we are united in our resolve to preserve our Precious Democracy, our self-governance, our Constitution of the United States of America. You see, while many of our neighbors have been voting for Republicans, mistaking lies for truth about what those Republicans will do for us in Congress, the ultra-wealthy, like Representative John Joyce have been voting against our interests. Representative Joyce voted against the Infrastructure Bill, the American Rescue Plan, the Inflation Reduction Act, and the Women’s Health Protection Act. Had more members of Congress voted like him, the sorely needed Infrastructure Bill wouldn’t have passed, but mercifully, Democrats carried the votes and Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District received the help it needed like lead-free pipes in Bellwood Borough of Joyce’s home county of Blair, updates to the airports in Cambria County, the bridge replacement in Franklin County, and rural broadband access in Adams, Bedford, Huntingdon, Juniata, Mifflin, and Perry counties. Recently, Representative Joyce voted not once, but twice for the One Big Beautiful Bill Act that not only removes healthcare from 17,273 PA-13 residents and food assistance from 6,626 PA-13 residents over the next ten years, but with the cuts to the National Institute of Health (NIH), loses 374 jobs and $86M from our district over the same timeframe. In other words, Representative Joyce does not care about us Everyday Americans - how could he? In a congressional district where over 46% of the public school students qualify for free and reduced lunches, multimillionaire John Joyce is clearly not one of us, but if he won’t have the decency to resign from his disgrace, then it is up to us, the Everyday Americans, to vote him out and vote me in. For when I take this oath of office, I will actually abide by it because I share a love of country with every veteran, every union member, and every activist who ever sacrificed in the name of Democracy. I will stand with my right hand raised and say with all of my heart, “I do solemnly swear (or affirm) that I will support and defend the Constitution of the United States against all enemies, foreign and domestic; that I will bear true faith and allegiance to the same; that I take this obligation freely, without any mental reservation or purpose of evasion; and that I will well and faithfully discharge the duties of the office on which I am about to enter: So help me God.” And I do ask for God’s help because I am firm in my faith that we should feed the hungry, heal the sick, house the unhoused, welcome the stranger, and help the poor. To stuff the pockets of the ultra-wealthy with tax savings that increase our federal deficit by $3 trillion dollars while depriving the least among us is well, not to put too fine a point on it, like trying to thread a camel through the eye of a needle. Instead, as a member of Congress, I will always check the illegal actions of a president who violates the Constitution by illegally impounding congressionally-approved funds. I won’t let a US President accept a $400 million dollar jet, much less for such an important vehicle as Air Force One. No due process for people suspected by Immigration and Customs Enforcement? Not on my watch. I will do my due diligence and vote FOR Everyday Americans, not against us. In other words, vote for me and I’ll vote for you. When our neighbors, friends, family, and coworkers who voted differently than we did in November of last year, bear the consequences of those votes through job loss, lack of funding, the rising cost of living, the depressed values of our assets, the losses of healthcare, the rise of poverty, and the blight that accompanies it all, they will start to lift their heads and seek real solutions. And in this constitutional crisis, we rise to meet that moment, If there is any message I ask you to please take away from today, it is simply this - Amplify this campaign. Because in the midst of so much darkness, we, standing together today, are the light. Like the torch in the hand of the Statue of Liberty, we are on the right side of history and the right side of humanity. We believe in healthcare, food assistance, due process, humane treatment of huddled masses, stewardship of the earth, public education, women’s rights, gun safety, and so much more. When yet another cloud of fascism threatens to dim our stability, I ask you to shine for our neighbors in PA-13 because soon, they will be looking where to turn, and we, we will light that path for them by our continued rallies, protests, town halls, doorknocking, calling voters, letters to the editors, reposting social media, or donations to this campaign and of those who also hold dear our same beliefs. When they understand whom to vote FOR, then they can finally understand that our US Representative can truly answer us, help us, and ultimately, represent US. And I say this not as someone who has lofty ideals built from thin air, but as someone who lived the experience as a former registered Republican who saw the light and voted against the darkness of fascism, suppression of equality, violence, ignorance, disease, and propaganda. When they join us in choosing me, Beth Farnham, then we all choose the best interest of PA-13 residents, the best interests of Everyday Americans, and ultimately, what the Revolutionary Founders gave to us - we too Choose Democracy. Thank you. Earlier in the week, I attended the Juniata County Democratic Committee picnic where I met with many voters and heard what was on their hearts and minds. Later, I spoke to them about my fight for Everyday Americans, including the rising cost of living due to an incompetent tariff-by-tweet policy and impending job, healthcare, and food assistance losses as a result of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act that Representative John Joyce voted for twice. Because part of the fight for Everyday Americans includes fighting for Affordable Housing, I attended the Landlord Summit on Tuesday. see, until Congress authorizes a livable federal minimum wage, Everyday Americans are stuck paying exorbitant costs in rent, mortgages, taxes, and/or interest. The cost of housing is so far out of balance with income that programs exist to offset this tremendous burden for those least able to afford it. Frankly, housing is a basic human right and so these programs shouldn’t have to exist in the richest nation in the world, but here we are. I truly appreciate the informative presentation that I, and others, received from Amanda with the Franklin County Housing Authority. Because of her vast knowledge and the knowledge of others who work with her, I, and Campaign Manager, Jack Stansbury, were able to learn about the Housing Choice Voucher Program from the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development, the Franklin County Community Connections Access Center, Service Access & Management, Inc. which also includes Rapid Rehoming, Bridge Rental Assistance Subsidy, Veterans Affairs Supportive Housing, and information about the Public Housing available in Franklin County. The hard work that many of these workers do to serve the community often goes unnoticed, but I thank them for giving me a greater understanding of the local resources available for families in need. Some people celebrate the One Big Beautiful Bill Act that a convicted felon signed into law on July 4th of this year in terms of housing. For example, (OBBBA) provides a long overdue expansion of the Low-Income Housing Tax Credit, which funds a majority of new affordable housing projects in the United States. Unfortunately, OBBBA’s “...$3 trillion bill’s devastating cuts to Medicaid and SNAP, enacted to free up funds for tax cuts for the ultra-rich, will bring about widespread job loss and a surge in poverty, causing the number of people who cannot afford housing to soar far beyond what the new housing provisions could address. (That is especially true in this era of surging construction costs thanks to tariffs and the decimation of the construction workforce.)” Moreover, “Making Medicaid harder to get will disrupt the process of getting people out of homelessness, notes the Urban Institute, as well as remove a major funding source for supportive housing. Medicaid cuts mean nursing homes will close, turning people out onto the streets, or sending them home to family caregivers...” In other words, depriving people of Medicaid benefits, SNAP benefits, Medicare benefits, etc. like our own US Representative, multimillionaire John Joyce, voted for twice, only adds to the workload, but with fewer resources, that we learned about yesterday. Of course, OBBBA can be reversed, but only by members of Congress willing to do it. I am one of those people, so please elect me, Beth Farnham, in 2026. Together, we and Affordable Housing! (photo credit: Jack Stansbury) If you’re like me, you love this country as much as I do and work to make a positive difference. In the midst of this Constitutional Crisis, please consider reaching out to your local Democratic committees or Democratic candidates so that we can strengthen our Democracy instead of letting it slide into fascism. We don’t just #ChooseDemocracy, we choose Pennsylvania, we choose The United States of America, we choose Humanity and I am deeply glad to work along side you. Sincerely, Beth | | | | | Beth Farnham for Congress © 2025. or . | | | | Get the Jetpack app Subscribe, bookmark, and get real‐time notifications - all from one app! | | | | ---|--- | | | | Automattic, Inc. 60 29th St. #343, San Francisco, CA 94110 |

body:
One man with a history of giving to far-right causes has contributed $10 million...

**$10 million**
That is how much one man with a history of giving to far-right causes has contributed to an organization already running paid advertising to defeat the Election Rigging Response Act.
**Six**
That's how many weeks there are until ballots drop in California and people begin to vote. It's also how much time we have to convince voters with ads of our own before it's too late.
**Five**
That's how many seats Trump asked Greg Abbott to find in Texas, and the number of seats we can flip here in California to nullify what Republicans have started.
**$3**
That's the donation Gavin Newsom is asking you to make today to help us win. We're finalizing our own advertising plans and need your help to go as big as we can; it's how we'll win this race.
[**If stopping Trump from rigging the next election is worth $3 to you and you can afford to make that donation today, please do it here. Unlike the opposition, the majority of our donations come from lots and lots of people contributing what they can.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/erra-em?refcode=em250814&refcode2=2494_4767699_jkA_1j&akid=2494%2E4767699%2EjkA_1j)

Peter ,
Donald Trump and House Republicans are on a nationwide tour trying to convince voters their so-called “Big, Beautiful Bill” is a gift to working families.
But here’s the truth:
✅ The wealthiest 10% recap the biggest rewards
❌ The largest cuts in history to Medicaid and SNAP
❌ Hospitals, nursing homes, and schools stripped of critical support
It’s a scam, but Republicans are betting they can sell it before families feel the damage.
That’s where we come in. GOTV PAC is working to expose this bill for what it really is and to build the Democratic turnout operation we need to flip the House in 2026. But we can’t do it without you.
Can you chip in $10, $25, or whatever you can today to help us counter Republican lies and power Democrats to victory? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email08242025&amount=25]

Our professional phone bankers specialize in long- and short-term GOTV campaigns, event registration, voting assistance in heavily gerrymandered states, fundraising, and call time management. Our mission is to flip red districts blue, any elected position from dog walker to Congress member.
Learn more at: gotv.vote [https://www.gotv.vote/]

name: Jonathan Nez
subject: we’re counting on you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jonathan Nez for Congress
body:
Hi Peter,

Today, I’m officially launching my campaign to become the next Congressman for Arizona’s 2nd Congressional District because I’m tired of seeing Eli Crane betray our neighbors by voting to raise costs, bury the truth, and take away healthcare from our neighbors.

As a man driven by faith, family, and love, my campaign is about bringing people from all walks of life together to create meaningful change for our district.

In 2024, our campaign delivered one of the strongest overperformances in the nation. We had over 78,000 grassroots online donors, and put AZ-2 on the map to the point that this is now one of the DCCC’s “Districts in Play” – meaning that winning this seat is critical to taking back the House.

Before I say more, I want you to know: the early days of a campaign can make or break it and we need to raise $104,319 to hit the ground running, fully staff up and start getting our message out. Will you help me start strong by becoming a founding donor to my campaign today? https://clicksp.jonathannezforaz.com/f/a/RRXMDQJhET2Hio42Xrop5w~~/AAPPABA~/9eocdv-WPnRigqwVQSUNUqEFi9pnIU8_ruK2wWYIyLKN-GjBvwPSV3w4yZ5k7zDrweR2ezCfcPdcqehvPbOHI97itsXakJe6pFGqa7aYiJ8dwWkbD2IhkqmjX5qliW_Tq1F1sNlIWjqUl1sO3KSJ8g~~

Following my graduation from Northern Arizona University, I was elected to the Navajo Nation Council to represent my community, and I’ve been in public service ever since. I subsequently served as a county supervisor and as Vice President of the Navajo Nation before being elected President of the Navajo Nation in 2019. I’m proud of my record of public service, leading the effort to save lives during COVID, and making major investments in clean water in Northern Arizona.

I’m running to deliver generational change, because we owe it to our children and grandchildren to leave them a future better than the one we inherited. Unfortunately, Eli Crane isn’t interested in improving the lives of our neighbors. His votes to cut healthcare and food assistance from our most vulnerable populations might go over well in the fancy suburb where Crane lives, but out here in rural Arizona, that dog won’t hunt.

I’m ready to show up in Congress as a champion for rural Arizona, but I need your support. Can you please chip in anything you can today? https://clicksp.jonathannezforaz.com/f/a/l3imIoBSn3Np9Fs1K-qmKQ~~/AAPPABA~/M8-NPTtywT_lTpdsL2gfGnzrGNEeUlozH4pxStPeUkVuh4WpOqoUeiBrsPaviC4mcrJIKXT56RXAE3nxeG7f9VokykpZ4uDdE9xQ-CYGPt_Ggsw32NQSLIVj3g80nTqfg6AYYkbwh3ZlELRn4jBghJFOQQHesVmBelg7zgUyiNVblS52Xkn-a4UxLReSobRs3l2Jye9kOH57M1t0UuCsAw~~

Our district faces real challenges: from the rising costs of gas, food, childcare, and housing to a lack of accountability in Washington. We need a representative in Congress who’s focused on the issues here in Arizona — not the partisan squabbles of DC. Someone who will put working Arizonans first, create good jobs, lower everyday costs, support our veterans, secure Arizona’s water future, and protect the right to choose.

As the former President of Navajo Nation, I’m ready to deliver on the issues that matter most to our communities, but I can’t get there without your help. Please consider supporting my campaign today: https://clicksp.jonathannezforaz.com/f/a/ttGppqHVVUplWIFXX81gUA~~/AAPPABA~/gw_6gCAJqlWhsufN3lpIMuwxcveny0kpjeP3LqkvTmJv1x2uxCNWDDeLS2tX7SDj3juaO9vYG0izZlORgLkaFlfG_oHt1g90zAX3uAlZ5EfferFAaAgpbwuRzVd1f8aTSOi5qj4vmEcc92LIg3jj6g~~

Jonathan Nez

name: Mikie Sherrill HQ
subject: Our opponent is catching up to us
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
Peter, did you see this headline?
Peter, did you see this headline?
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93720?refcode=em20250824-9314&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9314%2E614464%2EJ0tVN3)
**One poll after another shows our MAGA opponent catching up to us in this election.**
**[This race is closer than ever, Peter — and if we continue to let Jack Ciattarelli’s attacks go unanswered, he has a real chance of pulling ahead. Can you split a donation between Mikie’s campaign and Heartland Patriots today to help us fight back?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93720?refcode=em20250824-9314&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9314%2E614464%2EJ0tVN3)**
**[DONATE](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93720?refcode=em20250824-9314&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=9314%2E614464%2EJ0tVN3)**
Peter, New Jersey is not the safe Democratic state we once thought it was — in fact, it’s far from it. The last governor’s election here? It was decided by 3%. New Jersey’s margin in the 2024 presidential election? 6%. And some of the early polling in this race? **_A statistical tie._**
Right now, my opponent is flooding the zone all over New Jersey with false attacks on my record and our vision for New Jersey. **And the scary reality is that these attacks work if they go unanswered.**
But we also know this: when undecided voters learn who Mikie is, what she stands for, and how her policy priorities differ from her MAGA opponent’s, **_she earns their support._**
**[Peter, can you help us gather the resources we need and split a donation between Mikie’s campaign and Heartland Patriots to fight back against our opponent’s attacks and spread our message to undecided voters? This race is one of the most competitive in the country, and we need all the help we can get.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93720?refcode=em20250824-9314&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=9314%2E614464%2EJ0tVN3)**
If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:
[Contribute $10](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93721?refcode=em20250824-9314&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=10&t=5&akid=9314%2E614464%2EJ0tVN3) [Contribute $25](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93721?refcode=em20250824-9314&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=25&t=6&akid=9314%2E614464%2EJ0tVN3) [Contribute $30](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93721?refcode=em20250824-9314&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=30&t=7&akid=9314%2E614464%2EJ0tVN3) [Contribute $50](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93721?refcode=em20250824-9314&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=50&t=8&akid=9314%2E614464%2EJ0tVN3) [Contribute $100](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93721?refcode=em20250824-9314&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=100&t=9&akid=9314%2E614464%2EJ0tVN3) [Contribute $250](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93721?refcode=em20250824-9314&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=250&t=10&akid=9314%2E614464%2EJ0tVN3) [Give another amount](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93720?refcode=em20250824-9314&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=11&akid=9314%2E614464%2EJ0tVN3)

name: The Democratic National Committee
subject: If everyone reading this email pitched in $3…
body:
Every dollar we raise right now serves a critical purpose:
---
Derek, With so much happening right now, you may not think that $3, $5, or $25 could make a difference in checking Trump’s power. **But you’d be wrong.** Every dollar we raise right now serves a critical purpose: laying the groundwork to take back Congress. Investing in our state parties. Holding Trump and congressional Republicans accountable in the courts, in their own districts, online, and in the media — and making sure voters are hearing that message loud and clear. We are able to do this work because thousands of grassroots supporters just like you have chipped in what they could, when they could, to support our mission. Today, we’re going to ask you to join them — but first, let us tell you why. **Here’s what our path forward looks like:** It’s clearer than ever that we need to take back Congress and elect more Democrats who will prevent Trump’s worst instincts from becoming our new reality. To win the Senate majority, we need to build strong, statewide organizing programs in at least eight states — places like Georgia, North Carolina, Maine, and New Hampshire. On top of that, we’ll need strong Democratic infrastructure in districts all over the map, to win in 35 vulnerable Republican House seats. As you can imagine, all of that will take a HUGE number of resources. **[That’s why we’re asking for a chance to earn your first contribution of 2025. If winning back Congress is important to you, can you make your first $3 contribution today to help us keep building the strongest possible Democratic Party?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?refcode=em_20250824_4408_fr&amount=3&refcode2=4408_22930333_wg33To&akid=4408%2E22930333%2Ewg33To) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $3](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?express_lane=true&amount=3&refcode=em_20250824_4408_fr&refcode2=4408_22930333_wg33To&akid=4408%2E22930333%2Ewg33To)**

****
| **[Donate $100](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=em_20250824_4408_fr&refcode2=4408_22930333_wg33To&akid=4408%2E22930333%2Ewg33To)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $250](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?express_lane=true&amount=250&refcode=em_20250824_4408_fr&refcode2=4408_22930333_wg33To&akid=4408%2E22930333%2Ewg33To)**

****
| **[Other Amount](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?refcode=em_20250824_4408_fr&refcode2=4408_22930333_wg33To&akid=4408%2E22930333%2Ewg33To)**
---
****
Thank you for your support,
The Democrats
⛅To all Democrats: 🇺🇸
If everyone reading this email chipped in $3 right now, we’d be well on our way to building the resources we need to organize and reach voters all over the country.
But the truth is that less than 1 percent of the people receiving these messages pitch in. That’s why we’re counting on all Democrats who know how high the stakes are to step up in this moment.
[**Will you make your first contribution to the DNC today?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?refcode=em_20250824_4408_fr&amount=3&refcode2=4408_22930333_wg33To&akid=4408%2E22930333%2Ewg33To)
**[Contribute $3](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?refcode=em_20250824_4408_fr&refcode2=4408_22930333_wg33To&akid=4408%2E22930333%2Ewg33To)**

_If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: The message going into 2026
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
Instead of lowering costs, Donald Trump canceled Big Bird.

| | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/216?t=3&akid=2626%2E4032634%2Ec8zM-h)
---
One year ago, Donald Trump was running for President, and he made some big promises. Remember his promise to end the war in Ukraine “with a single phone call”? Did the Big Liar do that? Remember his promise to stop the war in Gaza? Did the Big Liar do that? Remember his promise to release the Epstein Files? Has the Big Liar done that? He was pounding one promise over and over again: Lowering costs “on Day One.” So let’s take a look. We’re at Day 216, and costs are up. Cost of housing: up. Cost of utilities: up. Cost of groceries: up. Instead of lowering grocery costs, Donald Trump is playing on-and-off, red-light-green-light with tariffs, and actually raising your costs. Instead of lowering the cost of your cable bills and utility bills, Donald Trump is approving multi-billion-dollar mergers so a handful of monopolists can charge you more. Instead of lowering the cost of school shoes and baby strollers, Donald Trump canceled Big Bird. **The message going into 2026 is that Donald Trump promised to lower costs on Day One, and instead, he has handed out special favors to billionaires and giant corporations while families’ costs go up and up and up. And every day he lies about it more. He hopes that if he lies enough, enough people will not know fact from fiction. He hopes that if he lies enough, we will give up. He’s wrong.** [**I’m calling out every lie and pointing out every fact about Trump’s economy so that voters know who is responsible for raising their costs, and who his government is actually working for. I’m in this fight all the way, but I need to know if you’re with me. Please make a donation to power our grassroots movement to hold Trump accountable.**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3128?t=8&akid=2626%2E4032634%2Ec8zM-h)

---
_If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically:_ |
---
| [DONATE NOW: $3](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3128?t=22&express_lane=true&amount=3&akid=2626%2E4032634%2Ec8zM-h)
---
| [DONATE NOW: $15](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3128?t=24&express_lane=true&amount=15&akid=2626%2E4032634%2Ec8zM-h)
---
| [DONATE NOW: $28](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3128?t=26&express_lane=true&amount=28&akid=2626%2E4032634%2Ec8zM-h)
---
| [DONATE NOW: $50](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3128?t=28&express_lane=true&amount=50&akid=2626%2E4032634%2Ec8zM-h)
---
| [DONATE NOW: $100](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3128?t=18&express_lane=true&amount=100&akid=2626%2E4032634%2Ec8zM-h)
---
| [OTHER AMOUNT](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3128?t=20&express_lane=true&akid=2626%2E4032634%2Ec8zM-h)
---

---
The reality is that Democrats are the ones who want to make life more affordable for working people. Democrats know that our babies are our most precious gift, and high-quality child care is good for our babies, good for moms and dads going to work every day, and good for the child care providers and teachers who work so hard. That’s why Democrats are campaigning all across this country on plans to deliver universal child care for every family. Democrats know that America needs more homes. Homes for first-time home buyers. Homes for seniors. Homes for renters. Homes for people with disabilities. Homes for veterans. Homes for people with no homes. Democrats have the plans to build more housing and we have the determination to get it done. Democrats know that we need a functioning health care system where no one goes bankrupt because of a bad medical diagnosis. Democrats want to expand Social Security, expand Medicare and Medicaid, and make health care more affordable for everyone. And Democrats will keep our rural hospitals open for business. **And how do we pay for this? It’s a question Democrats get asked a lot. And it’s a simple answer: Billionaires and billionaire corporations don’t need more tax cuts — they need to start paying their fair share. That’s why I’m fighting to make sure people like Jeff Bezos don’t pay less in taxes than the average Boston public school teacher.** [**If you’re fired up and ready to fight for policies that will make life more affordable for everyday Americans, please make a contribution of $28 or anything you can to power our grassroots campaign.**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3128?amount=28&t=14&akid=2626%2E4032634%2Ec8zM-h) Thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth
| | | |
---
| [DONATE](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3128?t=12&akid=2626%2E4032634%2Ec8zM-h)
---

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Introducing myself
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
My name is Abigail Spanberger, and I'm the Democratic nominee for Governor in Virginia's general election.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250824_2_spanberger?amount=5&refcode=em20250824-4008&refcode2=4008_400544_k2HG9t&akid=4008%2E400544%2Ek2HG9t)
Team, it’s official:
**My name is Abigail Spanberger, and I'm the Democratic nominee for Governor in Virginia's general election.** I'm ready to flip the Governor's office blue this November, defeat Trump's agenda at the ballot box, and get to work creating a more affordable Commonwealth for all.
But one lesson I learned during my years in Congress and at CIA is that a strong team beats going at it alone every time, so I hope you will join my campaign today.
**[Will you become a founding member of my campaign for Governor by splitting a $5 contribution with Deb Haaland? There's no bigger election this year than here in Virginia, and I feel the responsibility to set the tone by winning big — but I can only deliver if we raise the resources to air ads, hire organizers, and turn out voters.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250824_2_spanberger?amount=5&refcode=em20250824-4008&refcode2=4008_400544_k2HG9t&akid=4008%2E400544%2Ek2HG9t)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250824_2_spanberger?amount=5&refcode=em20250824-4008&refcode2=4008_400544_k2HG9t&akid=4008%2E400544%2Ek2HG9t)**
In a few short months, we can start Democrats on a winning streak by flipping Virginia blue.
It's not going to be easy.
Four years ago, we lost this same Governor's race by _two points_ and saw years of progress erased in an instant. And my opponent Winsome Earle-Sears has shown she will indulge in fearmongering and disinformation to keep Virginia under Republican control.
Your contribution today, whatever the amount, will help us rise above the attacks and build a winning campaign that reflects our shared values.
If you're fed up with the chaos coming out of Washington, if you're outraged watching GOP officials bend their knee rather than uphold their oath, I am counting on your immediate and full support.
[**Click this link to complete your donation and become a founding member of my campaign for Governor.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250824_2_spanberger?refcode=em20250824-4008&refcode2=4008_400544_k2HG9t&akid=4008%2E400544%2Ek2HG9t) Together, we will flip Virginia blue and send a resounding message to this White House and beyond that voters will not stand for the corruption, the attacks on fundamental rights, and the brazen disregard for the needs of working families.
**[Contribute »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250824_2_spanberger?refcode=em20250824-4008&refcode2=4008_400544_k2HG9t&akid=4008%2E400544%2Ek2HG9t)**
I'm honored by the trust you are placing in me. Virginia is where I grew up and where I'm raising my three girls, and I refuse to let far-right extremism win the day.
With immense gratitude,
Abigail Spanberger
* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
